name: Hajjaj ibn Badr
title: Messenger of Basra
shortDescription: The carrier of a letter from the Shia elders of Basra to Imam Hussain (AS), who stayed alongside the Imam after delivering the message and sacrificed his life.
fullStory:
Hajjaj ibn Badr al-Sa'di was a renowned Shia from Basra with great devotion to Imam Hussain (AS). When the Imam wrote a letter from Mecca to the elders of Basra, a group of Basran Shias sent their positive response to the Imam through Hajjaj ibn Badr.

Accepting the risks of this critical mission under conditions where Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad strictly controlled the roads, Hajjaj quickly traveled the long route from Basra to Mecca (and then Karbala) to deliver the letter of support from the Basran Shias to the Imam.

When Hajjaj presented the letter to the Imam, he decided not to return to Basra. Having found salvation in accompanying the Master of Martyrs (AS), he told the Imam that he intended to stay by his side and sacrifice his life.

On the day of Ashura, Hajjaj ibn Badr, as one of Imam Hussain's (AS) brave warriors, went to the battlefield with a drawn sword. Previously known as a trusted messenger, he proved his loyalty in the battlefield as well with a heroic combat.

After a fierce battle against the armies of disbelief, this loyal messenger was martyred defending the truth, and his name was registered among the great martyrs of Karbala.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى الحَجّاجِ بْنِ بَدْرٍ السَّعْدِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Hajjaj ibn Badr al-Sa'di.
